critical_issues.description: 62 files lack a '## Why' section explaining the conceptual motivation. This makes learning harder for beginners. These should explain the problem being solved, why the approach matters, and what learning goals exist.

24 files have @moduledoc entries that are generic, incomplete, or lack detail. Examples: just 'Documentation', 'Docs', or 'Module description'. These should be descriptive and explain the module's purpose clearly.

483 files lack a 'Project structure' section showing directory layout. Only 69 files document this. This is the MOST CRITICAL gap — learners cannot understand how to organize their code without this guidance.
5 files use 'defmodule Solution' instead of a meaningful name. Names should reflect the actual challenge (e.g., 'MdLite', 'RateLimiter'). This reduces clarity and doesn't teach real naming practices.
7 files contain stub implementations with 'raise NotImplementedError' or TODO/FIXME markers instead of complete, working examples. Learners cannot learn from non-functional code.

top_issues: ALL 79 files missing 'Project structure' section (critical for beginners learning basics)
27 files missing '## Why' section (worst in this level)
7 files with stub implementations or TODO markers
Some integration challenges have incomplete moduledoc

recommendations: 🚨 CRITICAL #1: Add 'Project structure' section to ALL 483 files (87% of codebase). This is the single biggest gap. Create a consistent template showing directory layout, file placement, and how to organize code. Prioritize 01-basico for learner impact.
🚨 CRITICAL #2: Complete 7 stub implementations. Replace 'raise NotImplementedError' with working code examples. These are 100% unlearnable as-is.
⚠️ IMPORTANT #1: Add/expand '## Why' section in 62 files. Use template: explain the problem, why it matters, learning goals, real-world use cases. Focus on 02-intermedio (27 missing).
⚠️ IMPORTANT #2: Fix 5 challenges using 'defmodule Solution' — use descriptive module names (MdLite, RateLimiter, etc.). This teaches professional naming practices.
⚠️ IMPORTANT #3: Improve @moduledoc in 24 files that are generic/incomplete. Each should explain: module purpose, key functions, when/why to use it.
📋 MINOR #1: Standardize documentation format across all 552 challenges for consistent learning experience.
💡 SUGGESTION: Add 'Learning objectives' section to complement '## Why' — explicitly state what concepts will be reinforced.
✓ VERIFICATION: Spot-check code examples from each category to confirm they compile with 'mix test'.

analysis_notes.scanning_approach: Pattern-based analysis using grep/rg across all .md files
what_was_checked: H1 title presence (all files must have title)
'## Why' conceptual section (should explain problem, motivation, learning goals)
'Project structure' section showing directory layout (CRITICAL for learners)
References to 'solution.exs' (should be 0 — should use real file names)
Stub implementations with 'raise NotImplementedError' or TODO
what_was_NOT_checked: Syntax validity of Elixir code (would require compilation)
Completeness of code implementation (beyond 'does it have something')

implementation_priority.task: Create 'Project structure' template and apply to all 483 files
Complete 7 stub implementations with working code
Add/improve '## Why' section in 62 files (27 in 02-intermedio)
